records.ety: [OE. handlen, AS. handlian; akin to D. handelen to trade, G. handeln. See Hand.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Handled (-d'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Handling (-dlng).]

defs: To touch; to feel with the hand; to use or hold with the hand.
quotes: Handle me, and see; for a spirit hath not flesh.
About his altar, handling holy things.

defs: To manage in using, as a spade or a musket; to wield; often, to manage skillfully.
quotes: That fellow handles his bow like a crowkeeper.

defs: To accustom to the hand; to work upon, or take care of, with the hands.
quotes: The hardness of the winters forces the breeders to house and handle their colts six months every year.

defs: To receive and transfer; to have pass through one's hands; hence, to buy and sell; as, a merchant handles a variety of goods, or a large stock.
To deal with; to make a business of.
quotes: They that handle the law knew me not.

defs: To treat; to use, well or ill.
quotes: How wert thou handled being prisoner?

defs: To manage; to control; to practice skill upon.
quotes: You shall see how I will handle her.

defs: To use or manage in writing or speaking; to treat, as a theme, an argument, or an objection.
quotes: We will handle what persons are apt to envy others.
They have hands, but they handle not.

defs: That part of vessels, instruments, etc., which is held in the hand when used or moved, as the haft of a sword, the knob of a door, the bail of a kettle, etc.
That of which use is made; the instrument for effecting a purpose; a tool.
